# flashblade_lifecycle_rule (Resource)

Manages a FlashBlade bucket lifecycle rule for automatic object expiration and cleanup.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "flashblade_lifecycle_rule" "example" {
  bucket_name = "my-bucket"
  enabled     = true

  # Apply only to objects under the /logs prefix
  prefix = "/logs"

  # Delete previous versions after 30 days
  keep_previous_version_for = 30

  # Abort incomplete multipart uploads after 7 days
  abort_incomplete_multipart_uploads_after = 7
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `bucket_name` (String) The name of the bucket this rule belongs to. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `rule_id` (String) The rule identifier within the bucket. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `abort_incomplete_multipart_uploads_after` (Number) Duration in milliseconds after which incomplete multipart uploads are aborted.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the lifecycle rule is enabled. Defaults to true.
- `keep_current_version_for` (Number) Duration in milliseconds to keep current object versions before expiration.
- `keep_current_version_until` (Number) Timestamp in milliseconds until which current object versions are kept.
- `keep_previous_version_for` (Number) Duration in milliseconds to keep previous object versions before expiration.
- `prefix` (String) Object key prefix filter for the rule. Defaults to empty string (all objects).
- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `cleanup_expired_object_delete_marker` (Boolean) Whether expired object delete markers are cleaned up. Read-only, managed by the array.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the lifecycle rule.

<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
- `read` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Read operations occur during any refresh or planning operation when refresh is enabled.
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import flashblade_lifecycle_rule.example my-bucket/rule-001
```
